title: Recall Vote

jokes.text: Yesterday Jaden Smith came in, and he was like, 'Look, I want to quit the music/acting business and work here with you.' And I was like, 'Jaden, be serious. The world needs you. You have a gift.'
jokes.setup: Tom describing his business success at Rent-a-Swag
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim that Jaden Smith wants to abandon his career to work at Tom's rental store
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom delusion - perfectly in character for him to fabricate an interaction with a celebrity. The specific choice of Jaden Smith and Tom's earnest response adds layers.

jokes.text: Get a big old mug of hot chocolate, put on my thinking PJs, and get back to you.
jokes.setup: Serious business negotiation with a formal offer
jokes.punchline: Tom's childlike decision-making process involving hot chocolate and 'thinking PJs'
jokes.explanation: Great economy - 'thinking PJs' is a perfect Tom invention that shows his immaturity in a business context.

jokes.text: Listen to me very carefully. No matter what happens, you will never acquire my thinking PJs or my YouTube blazer... Nonnegotiable.
jokes.setup: The lawyer mentions acquiring the thinking PJs
jokes.punchline: Tom's passionate defense of his ridiculous clothing items, treating them as precious assets
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character work. 'YouTube blazer' is a perfect Tom invention, and his dramatic delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Larry, everyone else was able to make it to the committee meeting on time. What's your excuse? / I was voting for you in the recall election.
jokes.setup: Leslie annoyed at Larry for being late
jokes.punchline: Larry was actually doing something supportive for Leslie, making her complaint ironic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective irony. Shows Larry's loyalty and Leslie's stress, though not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: There's been a mistake. This says that I'm in charge of the silly skeleton room, when really it should say 'the undead serial killer room.'
jokes.setup: Character checking their Halloween assignment
jokes.punchline: Wanting to upgrade from 'silly skeleton' to 'undead serial killer' room
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing someone's dark preferences in a bureaucratic context.

jokes.text: Plus, um... It's Kevin Pollack's birthday. That's something to focus on today. Who cares about the recall? It's Kevin's day.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to deflect from recall anxiety
jokes.punchline: Desperately grasping at celebrating a random celebrity's birthday
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie behavior - deflecting major stress with random trivia enthusiasm. Kevin Pollack is a great random choice.

jokes.text: Ron, can you put some more tiny marshmallow in my hot chocky?
jokes.setup: Tom dealing with business stress
jokes.punchline: Asking Ron to tend to his childish drink needs with baby talk ('hot chocky')
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Tom's infantile needs vs Ron's stoic presence. 'Hot chocky' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Maybe you should try taking a walk... Out of my office.
jokes.setup: Tom expecting comfort from Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron's fake sympathy turning into a dismissal
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - appears to offer help then immediately withdraws it. Perfect structure and character voice.

jokes.text: Ron! You're in Bloosh! / What? / Ron's in Bloosh? / Ron is in Bloosh!
jokes.setup: Tom discovering Ron is featured in a lifestyle magazine
jokes.punchline: Ron's confused 'What?' and Tom's escalating excitement
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Tom's lifestyle obsession and Ron's complete ignorance of trendy culture.

jokes.text: She spent four months living in Kate Bosworth's pool house.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining Annabel Porter's credentials as a lifestyle guru
jokes.punchline: Living in someone's pool house being presented as an impressive achievement
jokes.explanation: Good satire of lifestyle guru culture where any celebrity proximity becomes a credential.

jokes.text: She used to be the face of the Eagleton phone book. Then she moved to Hollywood to pursue her dream of becoming friends with a bunch of celebrities.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining Annabel Porter's credentials
jokes.punchline: Her 'dream' being friendship rather than career success
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of influencer culture - the absurd career trajectory feels real

jokes.text: Annabel says that buying an island is the only real way to know that your goji-berry farm is pesticide-free.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining lifestyle advice from Bloosh
jokes.punchline: The absurdly extreme solution of buying an island for organic farming
jokes.explanation: Good satire of wealthy lifestyle advice. The escalation from goji berries to island ownership is well crafted.

jokes.text: Someone's getting a new leather jacket. / I don't want a leather jacket. / It's for me. This is a great day!
jokes.setup: Tom celebrating Ron's success
jokes.punchline: Ron rejecting the gift, Tom revealing it was always for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom move - appearing generous while being self-serving. Good character contrast with Ron's indifference.

jokes.text: April, you seem depressed, and I would know. I spent most of last year being treated by Dr. Richard Nygard for my own emotional problems.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to help April with her mood
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his own therapy in an inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris oversharing. Perfect character moment showing his boundary issues while trying to be helpful.

jokes.text: Every year, we would dress up like demons and egg Larry's house. / That was you? / Larry, please. We're having a private conversation.
jokes.setup: April explaining her Halloween tradition with Andy
jokes.punchline: Larry discovering he was their victim, then being dismissed from his own complaint
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that Larry was their victim, followed by April's dismissive attitude. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: With scary, scary monster claws! [Chris making dramatic gestures]
jokes.setup: Chris trying to cheer up April like Andy would
jokes.punchline: Chris's enthusiastic but awkward attempt at being scary/playful
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment showing Chris's awkward attempt to replace Andy's energy. Moderately funny but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Nobody wants Gengurch-family-themed Jack-o-lanterns. / Gayle likes them.
jokes.setup: Leslie criticizing Larry's jack-o-lantern design
jokes.punchline: Larry's weak defense referencing his wife's support
jokes.explanation: Good Larry moment - his sad attempt to defend his work with the only positive feedback he has.

jokes.text: Why didn't we call it 'City Hall-oween'?
jokes.setup: Leslie stressed about the haunted house
jokes.punchline: Her perfectionist nature fixating on a missed pun opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - even under stress, she's thinking about optimization and clever naming. Great economy.

jokes.text: A poster announcing the new Lilith Fair concert. It's fake, but it'll buy us an hour.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining support items for Leslie
jokes.punchline: Using a fake concert poster as emotional manipulation, calculating exactly how long it will work
jokes.explanation: Great Ben moment - showing both his love for Leslie and his calculating nature. Lilith Fair is a perfect reference for Leslie.

jokes.text: It's loosely based on the Klingon greeting salute. I just learned that. And if I had known, I would not have agreed to it.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining their secret hand signal
jokes.punchline: Her immediate regret upon learning the nerdy reference
jokes.explanation: Good Ann character moment - her discomfort with nerdy references and immediate buyer's remorse.

jokes.text: Hey, Leslie, how about instead of slightly changing a lot of banners, we paint our toenails to look like pumpkins?
jokes.setup: Leslie stressing about haunted house details
jokes.punchline: Ann's obviously diversionary suggestion of nail art
jokes.explanation: Shows the support task force in action - Ann trying to distract Leslie with traditionally feminine activities.

jokes.text: Your brain is almost as perfect as your face.
jokes.setup: Leslie complimenting Ann's suggestion
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment structure of the brain/face comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - her over-the-top compliments with unintentionally weird implications. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Hey, Ron, baby, what are the hot deets on Bloosh? / Start over and speak differently.
jokes.setup: Tom using his typical slang with Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron's blunt rejection of Tom's entire communication style
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction. Ron's response is economical, in-character, and brutally efficient. This could be quoted outside the show.

jokes.text: Annabel, could I be more jels of your low-lights right now? I mean, O.M.Squee, talk about bangs envy.
jokes.setup: Tom teaching Ron lifestyle magazine speak
jokes.punchline: The absurd vocabulary ('jels', 'O.M.Squee', 'bangs envy')
jokes.explanation: Good satire of lifestyle magazine language. 'O.M.Squee' is a particularly good invention.

jokes.text: Oof, you got a long way to go, Swanson. / I regret everything.
jokes.setup: Ron struggling with lifestyle vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Ron's simple expression of total defeat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron line - minimal words, maximum impact. 'I regret everything' is quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Too close to call, a real nail-biter, anybody's game... These are three phrases you won't hear tonight, as Leslie Knope has been voted out of office.
jokes.setup: Standard election coverage phrases
jokes.punchline: Revealing they don't apply because the election was decisive
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection. Good setup/payoff structure, though not character-specific.

jokes.text: ♪ I got-a the pizza, pizza time, it's-a pizza time! / Larry, everyone is miserable, and you are only making it worse.
jokes.setup: Tragic moment of Leslie losing election
jokes.punchline: Larry's obliviously cheerful entrance with terrible Italian accent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry timing - his complete obliviousness to the room's mood. The contrast creates great cringe comedy.

jokes.text: I left her, like, 30 voice mails, and her outgoing message is just her sobbing and burping and crazy laughing.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining Leslie's condition after the loss
jokes.punchline: The specific, pathetic details of Leslie's voicemail breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in the breakdown description. The combination of 'sobbing and burping and crazy laughing' paints a vivid picture.

jokes.text: What's up, fartwads?
jokes.setup: Everyone expecting Leslie to be devastated
jokes.punchline: Leslie's jarring casual greeting with juvenile insult
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expectations. Leslie using 'fartwads' shows her unhinged state while maintaining her upbeat delivery.

jokes.text: Eat my shorts, Jabronies. Knope, out.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Leslie's concession speech
jokes.punchline: Her completely inappropriate, juvenile draft speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Leslie's broken state. The Bart Simpson reference plus 'Jabronies' shows her regression to adolescent rebellion.

jokes.text: And you know what I think it needs, actually, is a papier-mâché li'l Sebastian! [Beat of silence] What? / Oh! / I'm gonna go fall asleep on a bench.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting adding Li'l Sebastian to the haunted house
jokes.punchline: Everyone's horrified silence at the inappropriate suggestion, Ben's exit
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment. Leslie's broken state leads to suggesting desecrating the town's most beloved figure. The group silence sells it.

jokes.text: First of all, Mozambique cashmere is the new cast-iron stove.
jokes.setup: Annabel explaining lifestyle trends
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical comparison between unrelated luxury items
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist line satirizing lifestyle trend speak. The complete meaninglessness of the comparison is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Finally. / But my fave new item is a deliciously bold chair... / Did that woman call my chair 'delicious'?
jokes.setup: Annabel using lifestyle magazine language about furniture
jokes.punchline: Ron's literal confusion about calling furniture 'delicious'
jokes.explanation: Good Ron moment - his literal mind can't process lifestyle magazine metaphors. Shows the contrast between his world and theirs.

jokes.text: Scary stuff is invisible, Leah... Broken dreams, disappointment, achieving your greatest goal and having it all fall apart and knowing that you'll never climb any higher.
jokes.setup: Kids asking where the scary stuff is in the haunted house
jokes.punchline: Leslie explaining real adult fears to children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy. Leslie's broken state leads to traumatizing children with existential dread. Perfect character integration and dark humor.

jokes.text: Can we have some candy? / I'm gonna give you something sweeter than candy, Ryan... The truth. It's very possible that some of you have already peaked.
jokes.setup: Child asking for candy
jokes.punchline: Leslie offering 'truth' about them having already peaked in life
jokes.explanation: Exceptional cringe comedy. The contrast between 'something sweeter than candy' and existential despair is perfect. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Just remember, kids, nothing gold can stay.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying goodbye to traumatized children
jokes.punchline: Ending with a depressing Robert Frost quote
jokes.explanation: Great capstone to Leslie's inappropriate interaction with kids. Using classic poetry for existential dread is perfectly Leslie.

jokes.text: Even better, use it as a focal point in your yoga tent. / Put it by a table and eat a meal.
jokes.setup: Annabel suggesting elaborate uses for Ron's chair
jokes.punchline: Ron's blunt, practical suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response to lifestyle excess. The contrast between 'yoga tent focal point' and 'eat a meal' is expertly executed.

jokes.text: We don't do meals in my home. No, every two hours, we eat what I like to call a food tease, like an oat wedge or a seaweed lozenge.
jokes.setup: Discussion of eating
jokes.punchline: Annabel's pretentious micro-dining with ridiculous food names
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of pretentious eating habits. 'Food tease', 'oat wedge', and 'seaweed lozenge' are all perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: And I think this is really important, so I need everyone to shut up... I love your hair. / Oh. Thank you. / It's genetic and unattainable.
jokes.setup: Joan demanding silence for an important announcement
jokes.punchline: It's just a hair compliment, followed by Annabel's pretentious response
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Joan's dramatic buildup to nothing. Annabel's 'genetic and unattainable' response is perfectly pretentious.

jokes.text: I am scaring you! I could see you. There's, like, a million mirrors in front of me.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to be scary for April
jokes.punchline: His confusion about being visible due to mirrors everywhere
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy moment. Chris's earnest confusion works with his character but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: What about this? I'm Bert Macklin, friendly lifeguard. / Bert Macklin is not a lifeguard. He's an FBI agent. / Really? That's even harder to believe.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to channel Andy's character
jokes.punchline: April correcting him, Chris's response about FBI being less believable
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Andy's characters. Chris's logical response about FBI believability adds a nice layer.

jokes.text: Now I'm sad that I miss Andy and sad that I can't make fun of you to Andy.
jokes.setup: April explaining why Chris's attempts aren't working
jokes.punchline: Her specific complaint about not being able to mock Chris with Andy
jokes.explanation: Perfect April line - shows her relationship with Andy and her mean streak toward Chris. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: I've failed you, both as a scary monster and a friend.
jokes.setup: Chris realizing his attempts to help aren't working
jokes.punchline: His sincere assessment of failing at two distinct roles
jokes.explanation: Good Chris moment - his earnest analysis of failure in both silly and sincere categories. Great economy.

jokes.text: I was impeached when I was 18, and it was brutal.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to relate to Leslie's political defeat
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to his teenage impeachment as Ice Town mayor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ben's backstory. The casual delivery of such an extreme political failure works well.

jokes.text: Oh, God, did I peak when I was 18? / There it is. / Drink up.
jokes.setup: Ben comparing his and Leslie's career trajectories
jokes.punchline: Ben's horrific realization that he may have peaked as a teenager
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ben's existential crisis. Leslie's 'there it is' shows she was leading him to this realization.

jokes.text: B-13 shots. Bird bath salts. Champagne decanters!
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben drinking and getting increasingly drunk
jokes.punchline: Leslie's excited, nonsensical exclamations about random objects
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie getting drunk and silly. 'Bird bath salts' is a nice nonsense phrase but not exceptional.

jokes.text: When you talk me up, be sure to mention that you really admire how I take big risks with men's neck accessories.
jokes.setup: Tom coaching Ron before meeting Annabel
jokes.punchline: His specific and ridiculous self-promotion request
jokes.explanation: 'Men's neck accessories' is perfectly specific Tom pretension about fashion

jokes.text: If my chairs were mass-produced, they wouldn't be Swanson chairs. Swanson chairs are handmade. / Yes, and now they will be made by thousands of tiny Chinese hands.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his craftsmanship principles
jokes.punchline: Annabel's tone-deaf response about Chinese manufacturing
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Ron's principles and Annabel's commercial mindset. Her phrasing is appropriately tone-deaf.

jokes.text: Oh, no, I am not done with you, you wicked little so-and-so.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to leave the conversation
jokes.punchline: Annabel's inappropriate, overly familiar aggression
jokes.explanation: Shows Annabel's pushiness and inappropriate boundary-crossing. 'Wicked little so-and-so' fits her affected speech.

jokes.text: Hello, sir. We are sober. We would like to get tattoos on our bodies, 'cause we love each other.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben entering a pawn shop drunk
jokes.punchline: Leslie's formal announcement of their sobriety while clearly drunk
jokes.explanation: Great drunk logic - announcing sobriety while making terrible decisions. Leslie's formal tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Tattoos? This is a pawnshop. But, yeah, sure, I can do that.
jokes.setup: Drunk couple asking for tattoos at a pawn shop
jokes.punchline: Owner's casual agreement to provide tattoo services despite wrong business type
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment. The owner's casual acceptance of providing services outside his business model works well.

jokes.text: I want a portrait of Eleanor Roosevelt, tastefully done, and then she has a very classy tattoo of Pat Benatar on her arm. And that's very subtle.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her tattoo idea
jokes.punchline: The increasingly complex and ridiculous design she considers 'subtle'
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Leslie moment - the combination of historical figure, rock star, and her insistence on subtlety is perfect absurdism.

jokes.text: Do you have anything to numb the pain? / Sure, take a scoop out of the pill bucket.
jokes.setup: Ben asking for pain relief
jokes.punchline: Sketchy 'pill bucket' solution
jokes.explanation: 'Pill bucket' is perfectly sketchy detail for questionable tattoo parlor

jokes.text: Okay, you said you wanted 'mouth queen'? / No, stop. Stop it. Stop everything that you're doing.
jokes.setup: Tattoo artist confirming Ben's tattoo design
jokes.punchline: The misheard/corrupted version of whatever Ben actually wanted
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. 'Mouth queen' is appropriately awful and shows how bad decisions get worse when drunk.

jokes.text: What we are doing is not nuts. We are merely getting tattoos so that everybody knows we love each other and that we haven't peaked.
jokes.setup: Ann trying to stop them from getting tattoos
jokes.punchline: Leslie's drunk logic connecting tattoos to not having peaked
jokes.explanation: Good drunk reasoning connecting the tattoo idea to their earlier existential crisis. Leslie's logic is perfectly flawed.

jokes.text: Good-bye, world traveler. / Later, bro Heisen.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben saying goodbye to pawnshop owner
jokes.punchline: Their weird, overly familiar nicknames for stranger
jokes.explanation: 'Bro Heisen' is particularly funny - sounds like Breaking Bad reference

jokes.text: The only thing that's important at the end of the day is what's on your gravestone... Your name.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he values his name
jokes.punchline: His morbid but practical philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron wisdom - practical and morbid simultaneously

jokes.text: My gravestone's gonna be a 60-inch touchscreen with a hologram of four mes singing End of the Road by Boyz II Men... But point taken.
jokes.setup: Ron's gravestone philosophy
jokes.punchline: Tom's ridiculous over-the-top gravestone plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tom moment - perfectly excessive and specific to his character, hologram quartet is genius

jokes.text: Don't let her get a tattoo. / What the hell happened? / I couldn't help it. She's so cute when she's coming up with destructive ideas.
jokes.setup: Ben was supposed to stop Leslie from getting tattoos
jokes.punchline: His admission that he finds her destructive ideas endearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben/Leslie relationship dynamic. His weakness for her even when she's being destructive is very relatable and well-characterized.

jokes.text: They held the recall election, and I lost. I was voted out of office. In 30 days, I will no longer be a Pawnee city councilor.
jokes.setup: Ann forcing Leslie to read her own writing about the loss
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact, painful acknowledgment of reality
jokes.explanation: More dramatic than comedic, but Leslie's difficulty reading her own painful words has some humor. Important character moment.

jokes.text: Ann, you poetic and noble land mermaid, you're right once again.
jokes.setup: Leslie thanking Ann for her help
jokes.punchline: The elaborate, nonsensical compliment 'poetic and noble land mermaid'
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie compliment style. 'Land mermaid' is a perfect oxymoronic invention that became iconic. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: And if you want to egg Larry's house, come find me. / You guys are cute. / Where'd that come from? / I'm weak. I miss Andy, and it's making me weak.
jokes.setup: April and Chris having a moment
jokes.punchline: April's self-analysis of temporary weakness due to missing Andy
jokes.explanation: Good April character moment - her rare vulnerability and immediate self-awareness about it. Shows her growth.

jokes.text: Or he's secretly super in love with me. It's probably that. / That's a funny joke, April. But the truth is, I'm very much in love with Ann.
jokes.setup: April suggesting Chris might be in love with her
jokes.punchline: Chris's sincere correction and declaration of love for Ann
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction. Chris's earnest response to April's sarcasm shows their different communication styles.

jokes.text: Ron said there's nothing more valuable than my name, but he's not a businessman. I am. And now I have seed money for my next venture. I sold out, baby.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his decision to sell
jokes.punchline: His proud declaration of selling out, treating it as a positive business move
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - embracing selling out as a badge of honor. Shows his entrepreneurial mindset vs. Ron's principles.

jokes.text: She's never down for that long. And now that she's had a little time to recharge, she is like a toddler bouncing back from a nap.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining Leslie's resilience
jokes.punchline: Comparing her recovery to a toddler after a nap
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor that captures both Leslie's childlike energy and her resilience. Shows Ben's understanding of his wife.

jokes.text: Ann, it was very hard to capture your beauty, and, Ben, you make a sexy pumpkin... No surprise.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving jack-o-lanterns she carved of their faces
jokes.punchline: The casual 'no surprise' about Ben being a sexy pumpkin
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - the combination of complimenting Ann's beauty and casually sexualizing a pumpkin Ben. Good character voice.

tmdb_overview: Leslie tries to complete some of her biggest city council goals, like hosting an NFL Play 60 event with the Indianapolis Colts, and trying to add fluoride to Pawnee's drinking water. Meanwhile, Chris and Ron attempt to bond while building cribs for their future children.
insight: Recall vote chaos sustains 81 laughs through character panic, not punchlines.
